data.title: The Wellness Travel Launches Its First Retreat: South Reset - An East Coast-to-South Wellness Road Trip
data.description:
New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - February 15, 2026) - The Wellness Travel announces the launch of its first signature retreat, South Reset, an East Coast-to-South road trip experience designed for travelers seeking space to unplug, refocus, and return to daily life feeling renewed.

South Reset blends scenic driving with curated stops and structured wellness practices, offering participants a guided travel itinerary centered on rest, reflection, and simple routines. The retreat is built around The Wellness Travel's brand pillars: Reset. Rejuvenate. Restore.

What to Expect on South Reset

South Reset is designed as a supportive, intentionally paced travel experience. Program elements may include:

  • - A guided road-trip itinerary with planned wellness-focused stops

  • - Daily time blocks for journaling, mindfulness, and personal reflection

  • - Light movement practices and outdoor time (based on location and schedule)

  • - Simple meal structure and hydration guidance for ease while traveling

  • - Optional group check-ins and prompts to help participants stay aligned with personal goals during the trip

A Simple Nourishment Ritual

As part of the retreat rhythm, South Reset incorporates a simple nourishment ritual centered around bone broth as a comfort-focused, travel-friendly option. Participants may choose to include bone broth as part of their daily routine (for example, as a morning or evening warm beverage), alongside other foods that fit their preferences.

Follow the Launch

The Wellness Travel will share highlights from the inaugural South Reset experience across its social media channels, including destination moments, itinerary inspiration, and the retreat's daily themes.

About The Wellness Travel

The Wellness Travel is a wellness-focused travel brand creating curated retreats and itineraries that combine purposeful travel with simple routines. The company designs experiences that encourage travelers to slow down, reset their routines, and reconnect with what matters-through thoughtfully planned destinations, reflective practices, and a balanced approach to travel.

Founded by Oliwia Szczekot, a painter and lifestyle writer with a passion for holistic health, The Wellness Travel brings an artistic, intentional perspective to modern retreat experiences-where travel, creativity, and everyday rituals come together in a restorative way.

Upcoming Retreat: Beauty Inside Out (Los Cabos | March&May 2026)

The Wellness Travel's upcoming retreat, Beauty Inside Out, takes place in Los Cabos in March 2026 and is designed as a restorative escape featuring curated experiences, relaxation time, and guided holistic wellness practices.

Media & Contact

For updates, follow The Wellness Travel on social media. For press inquiries and retreat information, contact:

The Wellness Travel

book@thewellnesstravel.com

Instagram @oliwia_Szczekot

TikTok @thewellnesstravel

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/283928

data.title: New Zealand's a2 Milk posts 9.4% rise in interim earnings
data.description:
New Zealand's a2 Milk reported a 9.4% rise in half-year profit on Monday, supported by the robust performance of its infant milk formula product segment in its key China market.

Revenue from the firm's China-and-other-Asia segment jumped 20.3%, helped by growth in its English-label infant milk formula (IMF) and other nutritionals.

The dairy producer posted net profit after tax attributable to continuing operations of NZ$112.1 million ($67.66 million) for the half-year ended December 31, compared with NZ$102.5 million a year ago. That beat the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of NZ$104 million.

It also declared an interim dividend of 11.5 NZ cents per share, higher than the 8.5 NZ cents declared a year ago.

data.title: NZ's Contact Energy to raise $317 million for renewable energy projects
data.description:
New Zealand's Contact Energy said on Monday it will raise NZ$525 million ($317 million) for renewable energy projects.

The energy generator-retailer will use the proceeds to commence pre-final investment decision (FID) drilling on its onshore Tauhara 2 geothermal project, and to fund investments in its Glenbrook battery 2.0 and Glorit solar development projects.

The equity raising will be via a fully-underwritten placement of NZ$450 million and a non-underwritten retail offer for up to NZ$75 million, the company said in a statement.

Contact Energy added that it will issue around 60 million new shares, at a price of NZ$8.75 per share. This represents a discount of 8.8% to its last close on Friday.

The company reaffirmed its expectation of raising its total dividend for the full-year 2026 to 40 NZ cents per share, and between 41-42 NZ cents per share in full-year 2027.

($1 = 1.6567 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Freightways 1H Net Profit Up 17%, Upbeat on Outlook
data.description:
(19:55 GMT) *Freightways 1H Net Profit NZ$52.5 Million, Up 17% on Year
(19:55 GMT) *Freightways 1H Revenue NZ$718.2 Million, Up 8.5% on Year
(19:56 GMT) *Freightways Interim Dividend NZ$0.21/Share
(20:02 GMT) Freightways 1H Net Profit Up 17%, Upbeat on Outlook

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Courier and information management company Freightways said its half-year net profit rose by 17% as it expanded its market share and was able to lift prices.

Freightways, which delivers around 50 million items annually through brands such as Post Haste Couriers and Castle Parcels, reported a net profit of 52.5 million New Zealand dollars (US$31.7 million) for the six months through December.

Revenue rose by 8.5% to NZ$718.2 million. Earnings before interest, tax and amortization increased by 12% to NZ$96.5 million. Directors of the company declared an interim dividend of 21 New Zealand cents a share, up 11% from a payout of 19 cents a year earlier.

An improved result was expected from Freightways after management in late October talked up strong revenue and earnings growth from its Express Package & Business Mail division in the first quarter. Freightways said it had added customers, expanded margins and raised prices to cover costs.

On Monday, Freightways offered an upbeat outlook as New Zealand's economy emerges from a sustained downturn and interest rates fall, supporting demand for courier services.

"We expect a steady improvement in same-customer volumes in the second half of FY26, particularly in New Zealand, driven by a level of economic recovery," Freightways said.

The company said it achieved an Ebita margin of 13.4% in the first half, expanding from 13.0% a year ago. Forsyth Barr wrote in a research note this month that it expected a return to margin expansion after four years of compression.

"Margin improvement remains a priority," Freightways said. "Cost inflation remains moderate and the group's cost base has stabilized, particularly with respect to labor."

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Oracle-Anchored FX Liquidity in Practice
data.description:
A Full Lifecycle Case Study of the EURC/USDC Pool on Stabull

By Jamie McCormick, Co-CMO, Stabull Labs

The second article in the 15 part “Deconstructing DeFi” Series.

Following a measured fee adjustment in late December, combined with understated integration work with the broader DeFi routing ecosystem in early January, trading activity across Stabull began to accelerate — without incentives being increased, without coordinated announcements, and without any attempt to manufacture demand.

This case study examines that transition through the lens of a single pool — EURC/USDC on Base — tracing its full lifecycle from launch through to sustained execution, using only publicly verifiable blockchain data.

Protocol Context: A Deliberate Fee Experiment

In the final days before Christmas 2025, Stabull made a deliberate and measurable change to the protocol: the swap fee on key pools was reduced from 0.15% to 0.015%, representing a 90% reduction designed to better reflect real-world FX execution costs.

The reduction was originally intended to run through the end of January. It was introduced without a coordinated marketing campaign, incentive changes, or partner announcements — aside from a small update on Stabull’s own blog and social channels.

The intent was not promotional. It was observational.

Throughout 2025, Stabull’s on-chain history had already begun to show early hints of programmatic and solver-driven interaction, but always at modest scale. The fee reduction provided an opportunity to observe whether that latent behaviour would scale once friction was removed — and to do so in a way that allowed the market to respond naturally.

Discovery Without Fanfare: Aggregators and Routing

Around the same time as the fee reduction, Stabull’s integration with OpenOcean on Base went live.

There was no launch announcement or coordinated rollout. The integration simply made Stabull pools visible to aggregator routing systems already operating on the network.

Aggregators do not “send volume.” They surface execution venues to routing logic, which then tests, compares, and either incorporates or ignores those venues based on price quality, depth, and consistency.

The timing mattered. A major pricing constraint had just been removed, and Stabull pools had quietly become discoverable by routing infrastructure. What followed was not a spike, but a progression: discovery, testing, confidence, and finally sustained execution.

We’re grateful to the OpenOcean team for their support throughout this process. They have since deployed their Stabull integration on Polygon as well, extending the surface area through which Stabull pools can be discovered and routed to by the broader DeFi ecosystem. This creates a natural opportunity to observe how similar — or different — execution behaviour may be across chains under comparable conditions.

Market Context: What Kind of Trades Are Appearing

Before focusing on EURC/USDC specifically, it’s important to understand that activity across Stabull during this period has not been uniform.

Transaction graphs show a broad mix of interaction types:

  • Atomic swaps, executed directly in single transactions
  • Automated and solver-driven execution, appearing in clustered, directional runs
  • Arbitrage and cross-venue rebalancing, where Stabull is one leg of a broader execution path
  • Multi-protocol routes, interacting with Uniswap, Curve, Balancer, Morpho, and other DeFi venues

Although Stabull does not support assets such as wrapped Bitcoin or Ethereum directly, transactions involving ETH, wBTC, and other assets still appear upstream or downstream of Stabull interactions as part of wider execution graphs.

Taken together, this suggests Stabull is increasingly being used as execution infrastructure, rather than as a destination UI.

Why EURC/USDC Is a Useful Case Study

The EURC/USDC pool provides a particularly clean lens through which to examine behaviour.

It is a pure FX pair between two fiat-backed stablecoins. Pricing is anchored to a trusted oracle, volatility is minimal, and execution paths are straightforward to reconstruct on-chain.

For those reasons, EURC/USDC is not presented here as representative of all Stabull activity, but as a controlled environment in which execution dynamics can be observed clearly.

Data Sources and Methodology

All figures in this analysis are derived directly from public, on-chain data.

The dataset was constructed from token transfer exports sourced from BaseScan, covering all EURC and USDC activity involving the EURC/USDC pool contract on Base.

Only transactions with exactly three transfer rows were treated as swaps: one input, one output, and one protocol fee. Liquidity adds, removals, approvals, and unrelated transfers were excluded.

Volume was calculated using the output currency only. EURC outputs were converted to USD using the implied FX rate of each trade, derived directly from execution rather than external pricing assumptions.

All figures referenced below cover the period from 1 January 2026 through a partial 26 January 2026, reflecting the most recent data available at the time of publication.

The Full Lifecycle of the EURC/USDC Pool

Launch and Resting Liquidity (July–October 2025)

The Stabull protocol formally launched on Base in late July 2025, at which point the EURC/USDC pool became live.

In the months that followed, the pool entered a resting phase. Liquidity was present, but trading activity was minimal. Between mid-July and late October, approximately $149,000 of volume was processed across 198 swaps. Trades were sporadic, unevenly distributed, and lacked directional structure or market cadence.

This period reflects a common but often overlooked reality in DeFi: deployment does not equal adoption.

To ensure liquidity availability during this quiet phase, the pool was included in TVL-attraction campaigns on Merkl. These campaigns helped bootstrap liquidity so that routing systems could test against the pool, even in the absence of organic demand. Importantly, sustained trading did not emerge during this incentive-heavy period.

As incentives eased, liquidity naturally normalised toward the levels observed in January.

Latent Demand Under Constraint (November–December 2025)

From early November through mid-December, activity increased modestly. Approximately $350,000 of volume was processed over roughly two months, averaging around $6,000 per day.

This period showed early hints of automated interaction, but execution remained constrained. Trades were still infrequent, directional runs were absent, and there was no clear weekday or weekend rhythm.

The pool existed, but routing systems had not yet found it economically compelling.

Phase One: Discovery After Re-Pricing (January 1–14, 2026)

Following the fee reduction, January opened with a clear step-change.

Between January 1st and 14th, the EURC/USDC pool processed approximately $832,000 across 425 swaps, averaging around $59,000 per day — nearly a nine-fold increase over pre-January levels.

This phase reflects discovery. Routing systems began probing the pool more frequently, directional runs appeared intermittently, and execution economics were reassessed under the new fee structure.

Activity increased sharply, but remained uneven as confidence was still forming.

Phase Two: Sustained Execution (January 15 onward)

From January 15th onward, behaviour shifted again.

Through a partial 26 January, approximately $1.44 million of volume was processed across 772 swaps, accounting for roughly two-thirds of January’s total volume despite covering less than half the month.

Trades arrived in consistent, directional runs, clustered by time and block, with a clear weekday/weekend cadence mirroring traditional FX markets. This marked the transition from discovery to normalised execution.

January in Summary

From 1 January through a partial 26 January 2026:

  • Total EURC/USDC volume: ~$2.27 million
  • Total swaps: 1,197

January alone represents approximately 86–87% of all lifetime EURC/USDC volume since launch.

Capital Efficiency and Pool Turnover

With observed liquidity levels in the low-$30k range, the EURC/USDC pool supported over sixty times its own liquidity in traded volume during January.

On active weekdays, the pool frequently turned over multiple times per day. Weekend lulls and late-Sunday resumptions closely mirrored traditional FX trading rhythms.

Fee Generation and Native Yield

The pool charges a 0.015% swap fee, with 70% accruing directly to liquidity providers and retained inside the pool.

Using the most representative execution window — 19–25 January — the pool processed approximately $910,900 in volume. This equates to roughly $96 per week in stablecoin fees accruing to LPs.

Assuming an average TVL of $30–35k, this implies a native swap-fee APR of approximately 14–17%, with ~15% a conservative midpoint. These fees accrue in EURC and USDC and are realised on withdrawal.

A Conservative Outlook for February

To remain conservative, February projections assume no further growth, no increase in TVL, and no change in behaviour.

Using the late-January execution phase as a static baseline:

  • ~$910k per week × ~4 weeks
  • Projected February volume: ~$3.6–3.7 million

The reduced 0.015% fee, originally intended to conclude at the end of January, has been extended for a further month to allow OpenOcean’s Polygon integration time to feed through organically.

Whether execution on Polygon mirrors Base — or diverges meaningfully — will be determined by the market.

Verifiability and Participation

All activity discussed here is fully verifiable on-chain:

  • BaseScan transaction history: https://basescan.org/tokentxns?a=0x2a645d27efe5c8f5afc63856d2509ea20f81a0b2
  • Stabull Dune dashboard: https://dune.com/stabull_finance/dashboard#

Liquidity can be added or removed at any time via the Stabull interface, with fees accruing inside the pool and included on withdrawal:

👉 https://app.stabull.finance/pools/0x60a3e35cc302bfa44cb288bc5a4f316fdb1adb42?chain=8453

The pool is also included in an active Merkl campaign, providing additional incentives alongside native swap fees:

👉 https://app.merkl.xyz/opportunities/base/ERC20LOGPROCESSOR/0x2A645D27efe5c8f5afC63856D2509ea20f81A0b2

Closing Thought

The EURC/USDC pool did not grow through hype or emissions alone.

It progressed through distinct lifecycle stages — launch, resting liquidity, incentive-supported availability, constrained demand, discovery, and finally sustained execution — once economic conditions aligned with real-world FX behaviour.

What January demonstrates is not simply that volume arrived, but how it arrived: quietly, organically, and on the market’s terms.

Stabull’s role was not to force adoption, but to be ready when it did.

About the Author

Jamie McCormick is Co-Chief Marketing Officer at Stabull Finance, where he has been working for over two years on positioning the protocol within the evolving DeFi ecosystem.

He is also the founder of Bitcoin Marketing Team, established in 2014 and recognised as Europe’s oldest specialist crypto marketing agency. Over the past decade, the agency has worked with a wide range of projects across the digital asset and Web3 landscape.

Jamie first became involved in crypto in 2013 and has a long-standing interest in Bitcoin and Ethereum. Over the last two years, his focus has increasingly shifted toward understanding the mechanics of decentralised finance, particularly how on-chain infrastructure is used in practice rather than in theory.

data.title: Warner Bros weighs reopening sale talks with Paramount, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Warner Bros Discovery is considering reopening sale talks with rival Hollywood studio Paramount Skydance after receiving its hostile suitor's most recent amended offer, Bloomberg News reported on Sunday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Members of Warner Bros' board are discussing whether Paramount could offer the path to a superior deal, the Bloomberg report said.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

Paramount had enhanced its Warner Bros bid last week by offering shareholders extra cash for each quarter the deal fails to close after this year and agreeing to cover the breakup fee the HBO owner would owe Netflix if it walked away, even though the CBS owner did not raise its per share offer.

data.title: K33 Provides Trading Update for Q4 and Full Year 2025
data.description:
Strong year-over-year growth and solid trading activity into 2026.

K33 AB (publ) (“K33” or the “Company”) provides the following trading update for the fourth quarter and full year 2025 ahead of the publication of its Q4 and year-end report for 2025 on 26 February 2026.

The financial information included in this trading update is preliminary and unaudited and may be subject to change in connection with the finalisation of the full Q4 and year-end report for 2025 and the audit process.

K33 delivered strong year-over-year growth in 2025, with full year revenue increasing by 128% compared to 2024. Trading activity on K33 Markets has remained solid into the beginning of 2026, with 564M SEK traded so far in Q1 2026.

The full Q4 and year-end report for 2025 will be published on 26 February 2026.

Highlights

  • Revenue increased 14% year-over-year in Q4
    • Group revenue increased to 428,674 kSEK (375,432).
  • Revenue more than doubled in 2025
    • Group revenue increased to 2,211,330 kSEK (969,515), corresponding to 128% growth compared to 2024.
  • Q4 result impacted by non-cash Bitcoin revaluation
    • The net result for Q4 includes approximately 35,565 kSEK in unrealised loss related to the Group’s Bitcoin holdings.
  • 12-month rolling trading volume reached SEK 2.6bn (Subsequent Event)
    • K33 Markets’ 12-month rolling trading volume increased to approximately 2.61bn SEK as of 14 February 2026, up from 2.29bn SEK at year-end 2025.
  • 564M SEK traded so far in Q1 2026 (Subsequent Event)
    • Trading activity on K33 Markets has remained solid into 2026, with 564M SEK traded so far in Q1 2026, as of February 14, 2026.

Fourth quarter of 2025-compared to the fourth quarter of 2024-

  • Group revenue increased to 428,674 kSEK (375,432)
  • EBITDA amounted to -4,101 kSEK (1,349)
  • Adjusted EBITDA amounted to -3,545 kSEK (1,349)
  • EBIT amounted to -41,480 kSEK (-533)
  • The result for the period amounted to -39,752 kSEK (72)

The net result for the quarter includes approximately 35,565 kSEK in unrealised loss related to the Group’s Bitcoin holdings.

Compared to the third quarter of 2025, revenue and earnings were lower, reflecting normal quarterly volatility in trading volumes and client activity.

Full year 2025-compared to full year 2024-

  • Group revenue increased to 2,211,330 kSEK (969,515)
  • EBITDA amounted to -6,130 kSEK (6,967)
  • Adjusted EBITDA amounted to -3,134 kSEK (6,967)
  • EBIT amounted to -52,580 kSEK (-1,243)
  • The result for the year amounted to -52,173 kSEK (-4,178)

The development in 2025 reflects strong revenue growth combined with continued investments in platform capacity, product development, and organisational build-out to support long-term scalability, growth, and compliance.

Torbjørn Bull Jenssen, CEO of K33, comments:

“2025 was a year of strong growth for K33, with revenue more than doubling compared to 2024. While quarterly performance may fluctuate with market conditions, our underlying development remains strong, and we continue to see increasing activity from larger clients and institutional counterparties. Trading activity has remained solid into the start of 2026, with 564M SEK traded so far in Q1, with our 12-month rolling trading volume keeps growing. We enter 2026 with confidence, focused on scaling our platform, improving operating leverage and building long-term value for our shareholders.”

The Q4 and year-end report for 2025 will be published on 26 February 2026.

For further information, please contact:

Torbjørn Bull Jenssen, CEO, K33 AB (publ)

E-mail: ir@k33.com

Web: k33.com/ir

About K33

K33 AB (publ), listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market, is the new gold standard for investments in digital assets.K33offers market-leading execution, actionable insights, and superior support to private and institutional partners across EMEA. Mangold Fondkommission serves as the Certified Adviser for K33 AB (publ).

This information is information that K33 AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact persons set out above, at 2026-02-15 20:55 CET.

Attachments

K33 Provides Trading Update for Q4 and Full Year 2025

data.title: Contact Energy 1H Profit Rises, Seeks to Raise NZ$525 Million for Renewable Energy Projects
data.description:
(19:44 GMT) *Contact Energy 1H Net Profit NZ$205 Million, Up 24% on Year
(19:44 GMT) *Contact Energy 1H Revenue NZ$1.62 Billion, Down 5.3% on Year
(19:44 GMT) *Contact Energy 1H Ebitdaf NZ$500 Million, Up 24% on Year
(19:45 GMT) *Contact Energy Seeks to Raise NZ$525 Million
(19:45 GMT) *Contact Says Capital Raising to Advance Execution, Potential Upsizing of Renewable Energy Projects
(19:46 GMT) *Contact Says Capital Raising Comprises Underwritten Placement of NZ$450 Million
(19:46 GMT) *Contact Says Capital Raising Comprises Nonunderwritten Retail Offer to Raise Up to NZ$75 Million
(19:47 GMT) *Contact Energy Interim Dividend NZ$0.16/Share
(19:55 GMT) Contact Energy 1H Profit Rises, Seeks to Raise NZ$525 Million for Renewable Energy Projects

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Contact Energy reported a 44% rise in statutory half-year profit and said it is seeking to raise up to 525 million New Zealand dollars (US$317.1 million) to advance a suite of renewable-energy projects.

Contact said its net profit rose to NZ$205 million in the six months through December, from than NZ$142.4 million a year earlier, as strong hydro generation and the expansion of its Te Huka geothermal plant combined with a maiden contribution from the Manawa Energy business that it acquired in July.

Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, amortization and fair value adjustments to financial instruments, or Ebitdaf, rose by 24% to NZ$500 million in the first half. Contact declared an interim dividend of 16 New Zealand cents a security, in line with a year earlier.

Contact, which operates hydro, geothermal and natural gas-fired power stations in New Zealand, said the equity raising comprises an underwritten placement of NZ$450 million and a non-underwritten retail offer of up to NZ$75 million.

"Contact is taking significant steps to ensure its readiness to support New Zealand's growing electricity demand, with 3-5TWh of new grid demand expected in the next five years," said Chief Executive Mike Fuge.

The investments to be funded via the equity raise include a 200 megawatt battery at its Glenbrook site and the development of the Glorit solar farm on the Kaipara Coast near Auckland.

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com
